stories.title: The Dragon's Quest
stories.description: A young knight ventures into an enchanted forest to befriend a lonely dragon.

scenes.text: At the edge of the Whispering Woods, a young knight adjusted her armor and peered into the shadows between the ancient trees. The villagers had warned her — a dragon lurked within. But she hadn't come to fight.

Deeper in the forest, she discovered a clearing where fireflies danced in spirals. In the center sat a crumbling stone cottage, its chimney still trailing wisps of smoke. Someone — or something — was home.
A thunderous roar shook the canopy. The dragon burst through the trees — but it was smaller than she expected. Its scales shimmered like opals, and its eyes held not fury, but fear. It was hurt.

She knelt and removed her gauntlet, extending a bare hand. The dragon flinched, then slowly lowered its great head. Its breath was warm. Its eyes closed. For the first time in centuries, the dragon was not alone.
They left the forest together at dawn — knight and dragon, side by side. The villagers gasped. Then they cheered. Some stories don't end with a battle. Some end with a beginning.

stories.title: The Starlight Garden
stories.description: A child discovers a garden where fallen stars grow into flowers.

scenes.text: On the night of the longest dark, a child followed a trail of light through the meadow. Each step left a faint glow in the grass, as if the ground remembered where she walked.

She found the garden behind a wall of mist. Flowers she had never seen before — petals of pure light, stems of silver glass. And in the center, a star had fallen, half-buried in the earth, still burning softly.
She cupped the star in her hands. It was warm, and it hummed a song she almost recognized — the lullaby her grandmother used to sing. She planted it deeper, covered it with soil, and waited.

By morning, the garden had doubled. New flowers of starlight bloomed in every color she could name and some she couldn't. The child smiled. She would come back every night of the longest dark, forever.

stories.title: The Most Generic Anime Ever
stories.description: Every trope. No exceptions. Taro-kun's journey to become the Protagonist.

scenes.text: Taro Protagonisto was late for his first day at Convenient Plot Academy. He sprinted down the street with toast in his mouth — as is legally required. He had no idea he was secretly the Chosen One, the reincarnation of a demon lord, AND the heir to a legendary sword. He thought he was just bad at alarm clocks.

He crashed into someone at the school gate. It was a girl with pink twin-tails. 'Watch where you're going, BAKA!' she screamed, even though she was clearly the one standing in the middle of the path. Her face turned red. She had already fallen in love. She would deny this for exactly 47 episodes.

At lunch, a mysterious transfer student appeared. He had silver hair, a black coat, and was leaning against a wall with his arms crossed — the universal anime pose for 'I am important and brooding.' 'Taro Protagonisto...' he whispered. 'I've been waiting for you.' He paused dramatically for 45 seconds. It was deeply uncomfortable for everyone.

'I challenge you to a duel!' the rival announced. Taro didn't know how to fight. But then a katana materialized in his hand, glowing with blue flame. 'WHAT?!' screamed everyone in unison. 'N-NANI?!' added the rival, because saying it in Japanese makes it more dramatic. An inner monologue lasting twelve minutes explained that this was the Blade of Convenient Plot Resolution.

The fight lasted three episodes but can be summarized as follows: they clashed swords, there was an explosion, the rival said 'impossible,' Taro unlocked a new form by remembering a friend said something nice to him once, and the tsundere watched from behind a tree pretending she didn't care while clearly caring very much.

Taro won, obviously. The rival became his best friend immediately — as is tradition. The tsundere made him a bento box but insisted it was 'just leftover rice, don't get the wrong idea!' The narrator confirmed Season 2 would involve a tournament arc, a beach episode, and at least one hot springs scene. Taro still had toast crumbs on his face.
